summary:
The process of calcification starts approximately between ages 6-9 as part of early dental development. By this time, it sets up a timeline leading towards potential future extraction needs if impacted or problematic later during adolescence when actual teeth formation peaks closer to late teens into twenties. \boxed{Ages 6-9}

The summary captures only two critical pieces—the approximate start period for calcification by age six through nine—and its relevance linking back clinically toward understanding common issues like impaction occurring more frequently after peak developmental stages near teenage years. This aligns closely while condensing Agent1’s initial response down significantly without losing essential context needed medically regarding timing expectations versus practical outcomes related directly to patient care considerations involving wisdom teeth management strategies over different life phases.
